sections.paragraph:
ἐπιχρίω 1 aor ἐπέχρισα (s. χρίω; Hom. et al.; Sym. Ezk 13:10; 22:28; TestSol 18:20) to apply a viscous substance, anoint, spread/smear (on) (Soranus p. 75, 7; Galen: CMG V 9, 1 p. 136, 30; Diosc. 3, 25; PLeid X VII, 36; cp. Od. 21, 179 of a bow being prepared for service by rubbing with fat; Lucian, Hist. Conscrib. 62 of an inscription covered w. gypsum; Galen: CMG V 4, 2 p. 246, 20) τὶ ἐπί τι spread on or smear on someth. πηλὸν ἐπὶ τοὺς ὀφθαλμούς spread the moistened mud on the man’s eyes = (Jesus) anointed the man’s eyes with the moistened mud J 9:6 (v.l. ἐπέθηκεν, s. ἐπιτίθημι 1aα; cp. SIG 1173, 17=Dssm., LO 108 [LAE 135] in a report of a healing κολλύριον ἐπιχρεῖσαι ἐπὶ τ. ὀφθαλμούς). Without ref. to what was put on, in Eng. the customary rendering is anoint ἐ. τοὺς ὀφθαλμούς anoint the eyes J 9:11 (referring to the procedure vs. 6).—Of the stone that closed the entrance to the tomb of Jesus ἐπέχρισαν ἑπτὰ σφραγῖδας they applied seven seals i.e. wax that receives an impression fr. a seal GPt 8:33.—M-M.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Soranus Soranus , I–II a.d.—List 5

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

CMG CMG = Corpus Medicorum Graecorum—List 5

Diosc Diosc , or Dioscurides, I a.d. physician, also Ps.-Dioscorides after I a.d.—List 5

PLeid PLeid = Papyri Graeci Musei Antiquarii Publici Lugduni-Batavi—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Od Od , s. Hom.; VIII? b.c.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

someth. someth. = something

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

LO LO = Licht vom Osten—s. Dssm., LO in List 6

LAE LAE , s. Dssm. LO—List 6

ref. ref. = reference(s)

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

  1. fr. = from

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 387
